submission_id.description: Submission ID (docket number, accession number) is the primary key for correlating with regulator records; must be extracted exactly as formatted to ensure accurate docket tracking

submission_date.description: Submission date is the legal anchor for calculating regulatory response deadlines, review clocks, and compliance reporting periods; errors can cause missed deadlines with enforcement consequences

submitting_entity_name.description: Entity name is required for OFAC screening, KYC/AML compliance, and regulatory relationship management; errors affect reporting accuracy

submission_status.description: Submission status drives compliance dashboard reporting and escalation workflows; an incorrect status can mask deficiencies requiring immediate action

submission_type.description: Submission type routes the record to the correct regulatory workflow; misclassification leads to wrong review procedures and reporting errors

regulator.description: Regulator identification is essential for routing to the correct compliance team and external reporting system

submitting_entity_id.description: Entity ID (CIK, LEI, EIN) is required for automated regulatory lookup and entity resolution; errors break cross-system reconciliation

decision_date.description: Decision date drives compliance lifecycle close-out and regulatory timeline reporting; must be accurate

filing_period_from.description: Filing period start anchors the reporting period for financial and regulatory analysis

filing_period_to.description: Filing period end defines the scope of reported data; must align with the reporting period declared in the submission

material_change_described.description: Material change flag triggers escalation to legal and compliance review teams; false negatives could suppress critical alerts
